author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
vande vṛndāṭavī⋅candraṁ rādhikākṣi⋅mahotsavam
brahmātmānanda⋅dhik⋅kāri⋅pūrṇānanda⋅rasālayam

caitanya⋅caraṇaṁ vande kṛṣṇa⋅bhakta⋅janāśrayam
advaita⋅mata⋅dhaureya⋅bhārāpanodanaṁ param

guruṁ vande mahā⋅bhāgaṁ kṛṣṇānanda⋅svarūpakam
jan⋅mude racayiṣyāmi kalyāṇa⋅kalpa⋅pādapam

aprākṛta⋅rasānande na jasya kevalā ratiḥ
tasyedaṁ na samālocyaṁ pustakaṁ prema⋅sampuṭam

ayaṁ kalpa⋅taror nāma kalyāṇa⋅pādapaḥ śubhaḥ
vaikuṇṭha⋅nilaye bhāti vane niḥśreyasāhvake

tasya skandha⋅trayaṁ śuddhaṁ vartate viduṣāṁ mude
upadeśas tathā copalabdhis tūcchvāsakaḥ kila

āśritya pādapaṁ vidvān kalyāṇam labhate phalam
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅vilāseṣu dāsyaṁ vṛndāvane vane

sampūjya vaiṣṇavān viprān sarva⋅jīvāṁś ca nityaśaḥ
kīrtayāmi vinīto ‘haṁ gītaṁ vraja⋅rasāśritama
en_translation:
I offer my obeisances to the Moon of the Vṛndāvana forest, who is a great festival for Śrī Rādhikā's eyes. He is the abode of complete, nectar-like bliss that condemns the happiness of Brahman realization.

I offer my obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrī Caitanya, the shelter of the devotees of Kṛṣṇa. He is the supreme remover of the burden of the leaders of the Advaita philosophy.

I offer my obeisances to my Guru, who is greatly fortunate and the embodiment of the bliss of Kṛṣṇa. For his pleasure, I will compose this 'Kalyāṇa-kalpa-pādapa' (Wish-fulfilling Tree of Auspiciousness).

This book, which is a casket of love, should not be studied by one who does not have exclusive attachment to the bliss of transcendental rasa.

This auspicious wish-fulfilling tree named 'Kalyāṇa' shines in Vaikuṇṭha, in the forest called Niḥśreyasa (Supreme Good).

Its three pure branches exist for the delight of the learned: 'Upadeśa' (Instruction), 'Upalabdhi' (Realization), and 'Ucchvāsa' (Outpouring of Emotion).

Taking shelter of this tree, a learned person obtains the fruit of auspiciousness: servitude in the pastimes of Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa in the forest of Vṛndāvana.

Having honored the Vaiṣṇavas, the brāhmaṇas, and all living entities eternally, I, being humble, sing this song based on the rasa of Vraja.
